The customer is not your salesperson. Their job is to give you money. They're fulfilling that. It's not their job. They got other stuff to do. So those are two big reasons. The reason that a lot that they don't refer automatically that a lot of business owners don't understand is that it's a much higher threshold of risk and pain to refer that it is to continue to patronize. People will even continue to patronize businesses that don't do a very good job because devil I know is better than the devil I don't. It's inconvenient. It's habit. It's you know I stopped to get coffee just sit at a counter reading newspaper have some coffee I might want to hear this morning. And there's three people at the counter next to me and they're all talking about how bad this restaurant is. And they're all. And it's clear they go there regularly. And it's all I can do not to say you know. There's another one down the street. But but but see but what's their conversation about the place. It's all bad. Right. So the restaurants are great example because we have all had this experience. And that is there is a restaurant you go to regularly. It's probably a neighborhood place not a chain. You it's maybe not obscure. It's not you know and you and your wife love it. So you tell a friend about it. And they go there and have imagined or actual it matters not. Right. They have a really bad experience. They can move away. You can go seven years without seeing them talking to them having any contact with them except a Christmas card going back and forth once a year. You finally see them somewhere. The first thing out of their mouth will be to tell you about that restaurant that you told them to go to you told them to go to it. It was seven years ago. Sir Mace had that. Right. We got right. So that reminds us when we're about to make this mistake again. Right. Right. So the threshold for patronage and continued patronage is much lower than the threshold to refer. And then there's different. So there's referrals that only happen by by request. So you move into the area and you say where do you take your cleaning. So I'll answer that question. But I'm not running around telling people about my dry clothes. Right. You're not an evangelist. That's thresholds even higher. Right. Right. So the list of why this doesn't happen generally is number one. Some customers have no circle of influence. They actually don't have the capability to refer. Okay. The few friends they have think of them as a has an idiot. Okay. If they do refer a place everybody knows not to go there to go. Okay. So so that exists. Right. But that's a very small percentage of people in general and of people you would have as a customer. Second no motivation. Right. They're just they're not demotivated enough to leave. But they are not motivated in any way shape or form to refer. Right. And you think their their their continued patronage is a sign of enthusiastic zeal. Right. For which you are entitled extra reward. Right. And you project onto that something that you know really isn't there at all. Right. So no motivation. No system. No mechanism. No process for doing it. So even if I am inclined I really don't have a way an effective way to tell you about my Dennis dry cleaner. Yeah. CPA whatever. I'm not going to create it. That you can act. Right. And then last no express gratitude. So the person who does actually overcome all those obstacles and actively bring you somebody send you somebody and is then not recognized and rewarded. So most nothing. Perhaps a casual.
